steps.title: Scale the table to smaller numbers
steps.content: The bacteria counts are large, so measure $y$ in hundred-thousands of bacteria. The table becomes $(1,2.5)$, $(2,5)$, and $(3,10)$.

steps.title: Fit the exponential pattern
steps.content: The scaled counts double each day because $5\div2.5=2$ and $10\div5=2$. Working one day backward from $(1,2.5)$ gives the starting factor $2.5\div2=1.25$, so the scaled model is $$y=1.25\cdot2^x$$

steps.title: Solve for the target day
steps.content: $5.12\times10^8$ bacteria equals $5120$ hundred-thousands, so solve $1.25\cdot2^d=5120$. Dividing by $1.25$ gives $2^d=4096=2^{12}$, so $d=12$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The count reaches the target on day $12$, which matches choice D.
